data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:50:35.458Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like StartsWith and EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to write MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically want to use MSTest string assertion methods like `StartsWith` and `EndsWith`. Let me check what MSTest version supports these - in MSTest v3+, `Assert.That` provides fluent assertions including `StringAssert` or the newer `Assert.That.String()` API.

Actually, MSTest has had `StringAssert` class for a long time with methods like:
- `StringAssert.StartsWith(string, string)`
- `StringAssert.EndsWith(string, string)`
- `StringAssert.Contains(string, string)`


- `StringAssert.Matches(string, Regex)`

Now I'll write comprehensive tests covering various formatting scenarios using these string assertions, and set up the test project structure.

data.content: The `null` cases throw `ArgumentNullException` (a subclass), but `ThrowsExactly` requires the exact type. Let me fix those two tests:
data.reasoningText: The `null` case throws `ArgumentNullException` (which inherits from `ArgumentException`), but `ThrowsExactly` requires the exact type. I need to split the null case into its own test using `ArgumentNullException`.

data.content:
All **33 tests pass**. Here's a summary of what's covered:

| Method | Tests | `StringAssert` usage |
|---|---|---|
| `FormatPhoneNumber` | 7 | `StartsWith`, `EndsWith`, `Contains`, `Matches` (regex) |
| `BuildGreeting` | 6 | `StartsWith`, `EndsWith`, `Contains` |
| `GenerateSlug` | 7 | `StartsWith`, `Matches`, `DoesNotMatch` |
| `FormatCurrency` | 6 | `StartsWith`, `EndsWith`, `Contains` |

Key MSTest assertions demonstrated:
- **`StringAssert.StartsWith`** — e.g., phone starts with `(212)`, currency starts with `$`
- **`StringAssert.EndsWith`** — e.g., greeting ends with `,`, currency ends with `.00`
- **`StringAssert.Contains`** — e.g., phone contains `-`, negative currency contains `-`
- **`StringAssert.Matches` / `DoesNotMatch`** — e.g., phone format regex, slug character validation

The test file is at `tests/Contoso.Text.Tests/StringFormatterTests.cs`.
